body: PT Daya Inti Guna Yasa Tbk, the retail company behind Mr DIY, will conduct an initial public offering (IPO) of 2.51 billion shares, or 10% of its issued and paid-up capital, with each share priced between IDR 1,650 and IDR 1,870, potentially raising IDR 4.71 trillion in fresh funds. The company plans to allocate 60% of the IPO proceeds to settle loan principals, 30% for new outlet expansion, and 10% for working capital, with the goal of expanding its network to reach more customers across Indonesia.
title: MR DIY to Conduct Initial Public Offering of 2.51 Billion Shares to Fund Expansion Across Indonesia.

body: PT Adhi Commuter Properti Tbk (ADCP) aims to increase recurring income to 5% of its net profit in the next 5 years, with 50% of its 142.2-hectare landbank remaining to be developed as a revenue driver by September 2024. The company's recurring income sources include hospitality sector revenue from hotels and property services, as well as commercial field revenue from integrated station parking lots and development hubs, with Q3 2024 revenue reaching IDR 280.22 billion.
title: PT Adhi Commuter Properti Eyes 5% Recurring Income Contribution to Net Profit by 2024

body: PT Solusi Sinergi Digital Tbk (WIFI) has injected IDR 53.44 billion in capital into its subsidiary, PT Dharma Sinar Semesta, to promote business development, bringing the total issued and paid-up capital to IDR 55 billion. WIFI reported a net profit of IDR 152.07 billion in the first nine months of 2024, a 3.4-fold increase from the same period last year, with its telecommunication business line being the primary contributor to its revenue.
title: PT Solusi Sinergi Digital Tbk injects IDR 53.44 billion into subsidiary to boost business development.

body: PT Sinar Eka Selaras TBK (ERAL) has increased its ownership stake in two joint ventures, PT JDSports Fashion Indonesia and PT JDSports Fashion Distribution, by acquiring 89,250 shares from JD UK for IDR 89.25 billion and 4,949 shares for IDR 4.94 billion, respectively, gaining full control over the joint ventures. This change will consolidate the financial reports of the joint ventures into ERAL's financial statement, with JD UK remaining as a franchise business partner, and ERAL leading the operational sustainability and business development of JDSports Indonesia.
title: PT Sinar Eka Selaras takes full control of two joint ventures with JD Sports Fashion PLC.

body: PT Waskita Beton Precast Tbk (WSBP) has completed a debt-to-equity swap with 9 creditors, issuing 178.54 million new shares worth IDR 9.07 billion, increasing its paid-up capital to 55.08 billion shares. This corporate action follows a peace agreement homologated by the Commercial Court of Central Jakarta, which has helped to reduce WSBP's equity deficiency, which stood at IDR 1.28 trillion as of September 2024.
title: PT Waskita Beton Precast concludes debt-to-equity swap with 9 creditors via private placement phase III.

body: PT Summarecon Agung Tbk (SMRA) reported a net profit of IDR 933.71 billion for the first nine months of 2024, a 42.9% year-over-year increase, driven by a 45.9% rise in net income to IDR 7.54 trillion. The company's marketing sales reached IDR 2.7 trillion, 54% of its IDR 5 trillion target, and plans to boost sales aggressively in the fourth quarter to achieve its target, with several properties, including Vanica Residence, set for sale by year-end.
title: Summarecon Agung Posts 42.9% Increase in Net Profit for First Nine Months of 2024.

body: PT Honoris Corporindo Pratama transferred 1.91 billion shares of PT Modernland Realty Tbk (MDLN) to PT Panin Sekuritas Tbk, increasing its stake to 15.29%, while PT Sinar Mas Multiartha Tbk, the largest shareholder, bought 2.33 million more shares. Meanwhile, other notable transactions included PT Nippon Indosari Corpindo Tbk buying back 1.02 million shares, and individual investors and foreign investors making various purchases and sales of shares in companies such as PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk, PT Wintermar Offshore Marine Tbk, and PT Fast Food Indonesia Tbk.
title: Multiple investors engage in share transactions in various Indonesian companies, including PT Modernland Realty, PT Panin Sekuritas, and others.

body: PT KB Finansia Multi Finance, the managing company of Kredit Plus, is offering a IDR 500 billion shelf-registration bond, with proceeds to be allocated to expand financing capacity for its users, particularly in consumer financing. In H1 2024, Kredit Plus reported a revenue decline to IDR 1.03 trillion from IDR 1.19 trillion in H1 2023, mainly due to a decrease in financing receivables, but saw an increase in total assets to IDR 8.76 trillion as of June 2024.
title: Kredit Plus Issues IDR 500 Billion Bond to Expand Consumer Financing Capacity

body: Harita Nickel (NCKL) reported a 17.8% year-on-year revenue increase to Rp20.4 trillion in September 2024, driven by the addition of a new production line at its high-pressure acid leach facility, with nickel processing contributing Rp17.7 trillion to revenue. Despite this strong financial performance, Harita Nickel's stock has dropped 18.23% since the beginning of the year, reaching Rp835 per share at the Indonesia Stock Exchange.
title: Harita Nickel sees 17.8% year-on-year revenue increase to Rp20.4 trillion in September 2024.

body: PT Sumber Global Energy Tbk (SGER) has secured a contract to supply 500,000 metric tons of coal to Vietnam's Duyen Hai Thermal Power Company, with a potential contract value of USD 44.45 million or IDR 705 billion. This deal follows a previous coal sales purchase agreement with PT Merge Mining Industri for 2 million metric tons, which is expected to bring in potential revenue of IDR 3 trillion, adding to SGER's net income of IDR 10.88 trillion and net profit of IDR 565.16 billion as of September 30, 2024.
title: PT Sumber Global Energy secures coal supply contract with Vietnam's Duyen Hai Thermal Power Company for 500,000 metric tons.
